level_signals: Strong experience with product management and leadership
Thrives in a small, agile team: has initiative and desire for ownership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong experience with product management and leadership.
Strong experience working with data and metrics.
Experience with productivity and knowledge work products.
Experience with prototyping and data visualization.
Experience with building data driven flywheels for iterative improvement.
Thrives in a small, agile team: has initiative and desire for ownership.
The ideal candidate should have strong communication and writing skills, the ability to envision innovative technology, and willingness to reimagine work to be more efficient and creative.
Ideal candidates have a passion in a particular industry that they are domain experts in: in the application please highlight which industry you are most interested in innovating.

scope.team_size_estimate: small team (explicitly described as small, highly leveraged)
scope.surface: Perplexity Computer and core search/agentic AI product experience
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B, AI-powered search and agentic productivity
primary_metrics_inferred: User adoption and daily active usage
core_responsibilities: Anticipate opportunities for innovation and value in enterprise industries.
Envision new experiences and deliver a long-term vision.
Understanding of levers for product-led growth and retention.
Work closely with engineering and design to align product expectations and capabilities.
Work with research to evaluate and steer nondeterministic models into the high value outcomes for users.
Work with data and user research to understand quantitative and qualitative data.
Have conviction to make difficult product decisions in the face of uncertainty.

green_flags: Small, highly leveraged team signals significant PM ownership and impact
Clear product surface named (Perplexity Computer + core search experience)
Company is a high-growth AI consumer product with strong market visibility
Explicitly values domain expertise and passion, giving candidates a way to differentiate
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated — candidates cannot assess logistics
Qualifications are broadly stated without specificity, making it hard to assess bar accurately
Posting first/last updated dates not available — unclear how fresh the role is

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 4+ years requirement and leadership language)
scope.team_size_estimate (inferred from 'small, highly leveraged' language in JD intro)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
ambiguities: No location, remote/hybrid policy, or work authorization information is provided anywhere in the JD. Location type set to 'unclear'.
All qualifications bullets are framed without 'required' or 'preferred' labels — all treated as hard requirements per extraction rules, but several could reasonably be soft.
The JD intro says 'In 2026, we launched Computer' — unusual phrasing that may reflect an aspirational or future-dated claim, or a copy error. Treated as factual company context.
